title: Q2 renewal forecast lands within 2% of commit for the second quarter running
anchor: Forecast ARR at $1.8M delivered $1.77M, a 1.7% variance that sits well inside the trailing four-quarter average of 8.2%.
connect: Beacon Logistics renewed three months early at $280K, clearing the largest single renewal risk ahead of the July contract date.
body: Forecast ARR at $1.8M delivered $1.77M, a 1.7% variance that sits well inside the trailing four-quarter average of 8.2%. Beacon Logistics renewed three months early at $280K, clearing the largest single renewal risk ahead of the July contract date.
trace: Forecast accuracy improving significantly from 8.2% historical average to 1.7% in Q2, with early renewal removing major concentration risk.

title: Mid-market NRR climbs to 112% in Q2, the highest reading in four quarters
anchor: Mid-market cohort NRR at 112% versus the trailing three-quarter average of 105.2%, driven by Atlas Insights adoption spreading across 24 accounts.
connect: Multi-product customers in the mid-market tier expanded at 124% NRR versus 102% for single-product accounts in the same segment.
body: Mid-market cohort NRR at 112% versus the trailing three-quarter average of 105.2%, driven by Atlas Insights adoption spreading across 24 accounts. Multi-product customers in the mid-market tier expanded at 124% NRR versus 102% for single-product accounts in the same segment.
trace: Mid-market NRR acceleration coincides with Atlas Insights adoption, where 18 of 24 Insights customers expanded during Q2.

title: Multi-product customers generate 22-point NRR advantage over single-product base
anchor: Multi-product accounts at 124% NRR across 46 customers versus single-product at 102% across 74 customers, with multi-product share reaching 38.3% of the base.
connect: The NRR advantage widened from 14 points in Q1 when multi-product ran at 116% versus single-product at 102%.
body: Multi-product accounts at 124% NRR across 46 customers versus single-product at 102% across 74 customers, with multi-product share reaching 38.3% of the base. The NRR advantage widened from 14 points in Q1 when multi-product ran at 116% versus single-product at 102%.
trace: Multi-product NRR advantage expanding from 14 points to 22 points quarter-over-quarter, suggesting Atlas Insights creates meaningful expansion lift.

title: At-risk ARR pool shrinks by $90K in April as four accounts exit risk status
anchor: At-risk pool at $220K across 3 accounts in April versus $310K across 7 accounts in March, with no new additions to the risk category.
connect: Top-20 ARR concentration in the at-risk pool dropped from 5 accounts in March to 2 accounts currently.
body: At-risk pool at $220K across 3 accounts in April versus $310K across 7 accounts in March, with no new additions to the risk category. Top-20 ARR concentration in the at-risk pool dropped from 5 accounts in March to 2 accounts currently.
trace: Risk pool improvement with net 4 accounts clearing risk status, reducing both dollar exposure and concentration in high-value accounts.

title: Time to first value compresses to 23 days for Q2 mid-market cohort
anchor: Q2 mid-market new customers reach first value at 23 days across 24 accounts versus Q1 cohort at 38 days across 22 accounts.
connect: Q1 mid-market accounts with TTFV under 30 days renewed at 91% versus 74% for accounts over 60 days.
body: Q2 mid-market new customers reach first value at 23 days across 24 accounts versus Q1 cohort at 38 days across 22 accounts. Q1 mid-market accounts with TTFV under 30 days renewed at 91% versus 74% for accounts over 60 days.
trace: TTFV improvement of 15 days quarter-over-quarter, with Q1 data showing 17-point retention advantage for faster onboarding.

title: License utilization above 80% doubles in the mid-market tier during Q2
anchor: Mid-market customers above 80% utilization at 22 accounts in Q2 versus 14 accounts in Q1, representing a 57% increase in high-usage concentration.
connect: Atlas Insights adoption spans 24 mid-market accounts, with 18 of those accounts expanding ARR during the quarter.
body: Mid-market customers above 80% utilization at 22 accounts in Q2 versus 14 accounts in Q1, representing a 57% increase in high-usage concentration. Atlas Insights adoption spans 24 mid-market accounts, with 18 of those accounts expanding ARR during the quarter.
trace: Sharp increase in high utilization coinciding with Atlas Insights rollout and correlating with expansion activity.

title: Customer health reviews generate 8 expansion opportunities worth $340K in April
anchor: Health-review-sourced expansion opportunities at 8 deals totaling $340K, maintaining the March pace where sales accepted 5 of 6 opportunities.
connect: Outbound expansion acceptance rate sits at 41% versus 83% for health-review-sourced opportunities in March.
body: Health-review-sourced expansion opportunities at 8 deals totaling $340K, maintaining the March pace where sales accepted 5 of 6 opportunities. Outbound expansion acceptance rate sits at 41% versus 83% for health-review-sourced opportunities in March.
trace: Health reviews showing 2x higher sales acceptance rate versus outbound expansion, suggesting better qualification and timing.

title: High-touch coverage tier delivers 96% GRR across the top-ARR accounts
anchor: High-touch GRR at 96% across 22 accounts representing 18.3% of the customer base, versus tech-touch at 82% across 98 accounts.
connect: Q1 GRR by tier ran at 94% high-touch and 86% tech-touch, showing 2-point improvement in the premium tier.
body: High-touch GRR at 96% across 22 accounts representing 18.3% of the customer base, versus tech-touch at 82% across 98 accounts. Q1 GRR by tier ran at 94% high-touch and 86% tech-touch, showing 2-point improvement in the premium tier.
trace: Coverage model showing expected retention divergence, with high-touch tier improving quarter-over-quarter while maintaining ARR concentration.

title: Mid-market green health distribution expands to 78% in Q2
anchor: Mid-market green health at 78% coverage across 47 of 60 accounts versus Q1 at 72% across 43 accounts.
connect: Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% versus yellow-health at 69%, a 26-point retention differential.
body: Mid-market green health at 78% coverage across 47 of 60 accounts versus Q1 at 72% across 43 accounts. Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% versus yellow-health at 69%, a 26-point retention differential.
trace: Health score distribution improving with more accounts in green status, and Q1 data confirming predictive value with 26-point retention spread.

title: Enterprise tier GRR holds at 94% with 106% NRR across $432K renewal base
anchor: Enterprise Q2 renewals at 94% GRR and 106% NRR across $432K renewing book, representing the most stable segment for both retention and expansion.
connect: Mid-market GRR sits at 91% with 112% NRR, showing higher expansion velocity but lower retention floor versus enterprise.
body: Enterprise Q2 renewals at 94% GRR and 106% NRR across $432K renewing book, representing the most stable segment for both retention and expansion. Mid-market GRR sits at 91% with 112% NRR, showing higher expansion velocity but lower retention floor versus enterprise.
trace: Enterprise segment showing classic pattern: higher retention floor with modest expansion versus mid-market's higher expansion but retention risk.

title: Executive sponsor depth correlates with enterprise renewal outcomes
anchor: April enterprise renewals at 6 signed deals, with 5 showing executive sponsor relationship deepening during Q1 2026.
connect: Top-20 at-risk accounts with executive sponsor reviews scheduled this week: 2 of the current 3-account risk pool.
body: April enterprise renewals at 6 signed deals, with 5 showing executive sponsor relationship deepening during Q1 2026. Top-20 at-risk accounts with executive sponsor reviews scheduled this week: 2 of the current 3-account risk pool.
trace: Executive engagement showing as renewal predictor, with 5 of 6 April renewals having sponsor depth and risk accounts getting executive attention.

title: Small-business segment shows 86% GRR with 102% NRR expansion ceiling
anchor: Small-business Q2 renewals at 86% GRR and 102% NRR across $252K renewing book, reflecting the pricing and engagement limits at this tier.
connect: Tech-touch coverage model serves 98 accounts at 82% GRR, with small-business representing the majority of this coverage tier.
body: Small-business Q2 renewals at 86% GRR and 102% NRR across $252K renewing book, reflecting the pricing and engagement limits at this tier. Tech-touch coverage model serves 98 accounts at 82% GRR, with small-business representing the majority of this coverage tier.
trace: Small-business segment hitting the expected floor for both retention and expansion, consistent with tech-touch coverage economics.

title: New customer 90-day retention improves 5 points with faster onboarding
anchor: Q1 new-customer cohort at 88% 90-day retention with 26-day TTFV across 60 accounts versus Q4 cohort at 83% with 38-day TTFV across 55 accounts.
connect: Mid-market Q2 cohort TTFV compresses further to 23 days across 24 accounts, suggesting continued onboarding acceleration.
body: Q1 new-customer cohort at 88% 90-day retention with 26-day TTFV across 60 accounts versus Q4 cohort at 83% with 38-day TTFV across 55 accounts. Mid-market Q2 cohort TTFV compresses further to 23 days across 24 accounts, suggesting continued onboarding acceleration.
trace: Clear correlation between faster TTFV and early retention, with Q2 mid-market showing continued improvement in onboarding speed.

title: Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15 ahead of Beacon's renewal expansion
anchor: Feature launch scheduled for 2026-06-15 with Beacon Logistics highlighting custom permissions during their early renewal discussion in April.
connect: Beacon renewed early at $280K on April 21, moving their renewal date from July 15 to eliminate the feature-launch timing risk.
body: Feature launch scheduled for 2026-06-15 with Beacon Logistics highlighting custom permissions during their early renewal discussion in April. Beacon renewed early at $280K on April 21, moving their renewal date from July 15 to eliminate the feature-launch timing risk.
trace: Product launch timing creating early renewal opportunity, with Beacon avoiding the June launch window by signing 3 months ahead of contract end.

title: Atlas Insights drives 75% expansion rate among mid-market adopters
anchor: Atlas Insights adoption across 24 mid-market accounts with 18 expanding ARR during Q2, representing a 75% expansion conversion rate.
connect: Multi-product customers overall show 124% NRR versus 102% for single-product accounts across the full customer base.
body: Atlas Insights adoption across 24 mid-market accounts with 18 expanding ARR during Q2, representing a 75% expansion conversion rate. Multi-product customers overall show 124% NRR versus 102% for single-product accounts across the full customer base.
trace: Atlas Insights showing strong expansion correlation with 75% of adopters expanding and contributing to multi-product NRR advantage.
